description_long: This is a military-style, single-propeller airplane with a dark green and brown camouflage paint scheme and prominent Polish Air Force insignia on the wings and tail. The fuselage is streamlined, with a long nose housing a single piston engine and a two-blade propeller at the front. The cockpit is enclosed with a clear canopy, positioned just behind the engine, and appears to be a single-seat configuration. The wings are straight and low-mounted, with visible ailerons and flaps, and the tail section features a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The landing gear is of the taildragger type, with two main wheels under the wings and a smaller tail wheel. The airplane also has visible antenna wires stretching from the tail to the fuselage, adding to its vintage military appearance.
description: A vintage, single-propeller military airplane with green-brown camouflage, Polish insignia, a low-mounted straight wing, and a taildragger landing gear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, propeller, cockpit canopy, and tail with Polish insignia.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the straight wings, taildragger landing gear, and the cockpit canopy.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the propeller, cockpit, and tail details, with visible antenna wires.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the propeller, nose, cockpit canopy, and the symmetrical wing layout.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single piston engine mounted in the nose with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main wheels and a single tail wheel, fixed gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy.
